info.title: elmahio
info.description: A CLI to execute common tasks against elmah.io.

x-inspectra.cliParsedDescription: CLI for executing various actions against elmah.io

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --version
options.description: Show version information

options.name: --nologo
options.description: Doesn't display the startup banner or the copyright message [default: False]

commands.name: login
commands.description: Authenticate with elmah.io and store your API key locally

options.name: --apiKey
options.description: An API key with permission to execute commands

options.name: --proxyHost
options.description: A hostname or IP for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

options.name: --proxyPort
options.description: A port number for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

commands.name: logout
commands.description: Remove the locally stored elmah.io API key

commands.name: deployments
commands.description: Work with deployments

commands.name: create
commands.description: Create a new deployment

options.name: --apiKey
options.description: An API key with permission to execute the command. If omitted, the key stored via 'elmahio login' is used.

options.name: --version
options.description: The version number of this deployment

options.name: --created
options.description: When was this deployment created in UTC

options.name: --description
options.description: Description of this deployment

options.name: --userName
options.description: The name of the person responsible for creating this deployment

options.name: --userEmail
options.description: The email of the person responsible for creating this deployment

options.name: --logId
options.description: The ID of a log if this deployment is specific to a single log

options.name: --proxyHost
options.description: A hostname or IP for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

options.name: --proxyPort
options.description: A port number for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

commands.name: list
commands.description: List recent deployments

options.name: --apiKey
options.description: An API key with permission to execute the command. If omitted, the key stored via 'elmahio login' is used.

options.name: --logId
options.description: Filter deployments to a specific log ID

options.name: --count
options.description: Number of deployments to return (max 25) [default: 5]

options.name: --json
options.description: Output results as JSON instead of formatted text [default: False]

options.name: --proxyHost
options.description: A hostname or IP for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

options.name: --proxyPort
options.description: A port number for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

commands.name: diagnose
commands.description: Diagnose potential problems with an elmah.io installation

options.name: --directory
options.description: The root directory to check [default: <path>]

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Output verbose diagnostics to help debug problems [default: False]

commands.name: logs
commands.description: Work with logs

commands.name: clear
commands.description: Delete one or more messages from a log

options.name: --apiKey
options.description: An API key with permission to execute the command. If omitted, the key stored via 'elmahio login' is used.

options.name: --logId
options.description: The log ID of the log to clear messages

options.name: --query
options.description: Clear messages matching this query (use * for all messages)

options.name: --from
options.description: Optional date and time to clear messages from

options.name: --to
options.description: Optional date and time to clear messages to

options.name: --proxyHost
options.description: A hostname or IP for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

options.name: --proxyPort
options.description: A port number for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

commands.name: dataloader
commands.description: Load 50 log messages into the specified log

options.name: --apiKey
options.description: An API key with permission to execute the command. If omitted, the key stored via 'elmahio login' is used.

options.name: --logId
options.description: The log ID of the log to import messages into

options.name: --proxyHost
options.description: A hostname or IP for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

options.name: --proxyPort
options.description: A port number for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

commands.name: export
commands.description: Export log messages from a specified log

options.name: --apiKey
options.description: An API key with permission to execute the command. If omitted, the key stored via 'elmahio login' is used.

options.name: --logId
options.description: The ID of the log to export messages from

options.name: --dateFrom
options.description: Defines the Date from which the logs start. Ex. " --dateFrom 2026-03-28"

options.name: --dateTo
options.description: Defines the Date from which the logs end. Ex. " --dateTo 2026-04-04"

options.name: --filename
options.description: Defines the path and filename of the file to export to. Ex. " --filename C:\myDirectory\myFile.json" or " --filename myFile.csv"

options.name: --query
options.description: Defines the query that is passed to the API [default: *]

options.name: --includeHeaders
options.description: Include headers, cookies, etc. in output (will take longer to export) [default: False]

options.name: --format
options.description: The format to export [default: Json]

options.name: --proxyHost
options.description: A hostname or IP for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

options.name: --proxyPort
options.description: A port number for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

commands.name: get
commands.description: Get details for a specific log

options.name: --apiKey
options.description: An API key with permission to execute the command. If omitted, the key stored via 'elmahio login' is used.

options.name: --logId
options.description: The ID of the log to fetch details for

options.name: --json
options.description: Output results as JSON instead of formatted text [default: False]

options.name: --proxyHost
options.description: A hostname or IP for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

options.name: --proxyPort
options.description: A port number for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

commands.name: import
commands.description: Import log messages to a specified log

options.name: --apiKey
options.description: An API key with permission to execute the command. If omitted, the key stored via 'elmahio login' is used.

options.name: --logId
options.description: The ID of the log to import messages to

options.name: --type
options.description: The type of log file to import. Use 'w3c' for W3C Extended Log File Format and 'iis' for IIS Log File Format

options.name: --filename
options.description: Defines the path and filename of the file to import from. Ex. " --filename C:\myDirectory\log.txt"

options.name: --dateFrom
options.description: Defines the Date from which the logs start. Ex. " --dateFrom 2026-03-28"

options.name: --dateTo
options.description: Defines the Date from which the logs end. Ex. " --dateTo 2026-04-04"

options.name: --proxyHost
options.description: A hostname or IP for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

options.name: --proxyPort
options.description: A port number for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

commands.name: list
commands.description: List all logs accessible with the API key

options.name: --apiKey
options.description: An API key with permission to execute the command. If omitted, the key stored via 'elmahio login' is used.

options.name: --environment
options.description: Filter logs by environment name

options.name: --json
options.description: Output results as JSON instead of formatted text [default: False]

options.name: --proxyHost
options.description: A hostname or IP for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

options.name: --proxyPort
options.description: A port number for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

commands.name: sourcemap
commands.description: Upload a source map and minified JavaScript

options.name: --apiKey
options.description: An API key with permission to execute the command. If omitted, the key stored via 'elmahio login' is used.

options.name: --logId
options.description: The ID of the log which should contain the minified JavaScript and source map

options.name: --path
options.description: An URL to the online minified JavaScript file

options.name: --sourceMap
options.description: The source map file. Only files with an extension of .map and content type of application/json will be accepted

options.name: --minifiedJavaScript
options.description: The minified JavaScript file. Only files with an extension of .js and content type of text/javascript will be accepted

options.name: --proxyHost
options.description: A hostname or IP for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

options.name: --proxyPort
options.description: A port number for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

commands.name: tail
commands.description: Tail log messages from a specified log

options.name: --apiKey
options.description: An API key with permission to execute the command. If omitted, the key stored via 'elmahio login' is used.

options.name: --logId
options.description: The ID of the log to send the log message to

options.name: --proxyHost
options.description: A hostname or IP for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

options.name: --proxyPort
options.description: A port number for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

commands.name: messages
commands.description: Work with log messages

commands.name: count
commands.description: Count log messages matching optional filters

options.name: --apiKey
options.description: An API key with permission to execute the command. If omitted, the key stored via 'elmahio login' is used.

options.name: --logId
options.description: The ID of the log

options.name: --query
options.description: Full-text or Lucene query to filter messages

options.name: --severity
options.description: Filter by severity (Verbose, Debug, Information, Warning, Error, Fatal)

options.name: --from
options.description: Count messages from this date (defaults to 90 days ago)

options.name: --json
options.description: Output results as JSON instead of formatted text [default: False]

options.name: --proxyHost
options.description: A hostname or IP for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

options.name: --proxyPort
options.description: A port number for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

commands.name: get
commands.description: Fetch a single log message by ID

options.name: --apiKey
options.description: An API key with permission to execute the command. If omitted, the key stored via 'elmahio login' is used.

options.name: --logId
options.description: The ID of the log

options.name: --messageId
options.description: The ID of the message to fetch

options.name: --json
options.description: Output results as JSON instead of formatted text [default: False]

options.name: --proxyHost
options.description: A hostname or IP for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

options.name: --proxyPort
options.description: A port number for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

commands.name: list-frequent
commands.description: List the most frequently occurring error groups

options.name: --apiKey
options.description: An API key with permission to execute the command. If omitted, the key stored via 'elmahio login' is used.

options.name: --logId
options.description: The ID of the log

options.name: --count
options.description: Number of frequent groups to return (1-25) [default: 5]

options.name: --query
options.description: Full-text or Lucene query to filter messages

options.name: --severity
options.description: Filter by severity (Verbose, Debug, Information, Warning, Error, Fatal) [default: Error]

options.name: --from
options.description: Search from this date

options.name: --to
options.description: Search to this date

options.name: --json
options.description: Output results as JSON instead of formatted text [default: False]

options.name: --proxyHost
options.description: A hostname or IP for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

options.name: --proxyPort
options.description: A port number for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

commands.name: list-recent
commands.description: List the most recent log messages

options.name: --apiKey
options.description: An API key with permission to execute the command. If omitted, the key stored via 'elmahio login' is used.

options.name: --logId
options.description: The ID of the log

options.name: --count
options.description: Number of messages to return (1-100) [default: 10]

options.name: --query
options.description: Full-text or Lucene query to filter messages

options.name: --severity
options.description: Filter by severity (Verbose, Debug, Information, Warning, Error, Fatal) [default: Error]

options.name: --from
options.description: Return messages from this date

options.name: --to
options.description: Return messages up to this date

options.name: --json
options.description: Output results as JSON instead of formatted text [default: False]

options.name: --proxyHost
options.description: A hostname or IP for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

options.name: --proxyPort
options.description: A port number for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

commands.name: log
commands.description: Log a message to the specified log

options.name: --apiKey
options.description: An API key with permission to execute the command. If omitted, the key stored via 'elmahio login' is used.

options.name: --logId
options.description: The ID of the log to send the log message to

options.name: --application
options.description: Used to identify which application logged this message. You can use this if you have multiple applications and services logging to the same log

options.name: --detail
options.description: A longer description of the message. For errors this could be a stacktrace, but it's really up to you what to log in there.

options.name: --hostname
options.description: The hostname of the server logging the message.

options.name: --title
options.description: The textual title or headline of the message to log.

options.name: --titleTemplate
options.description: The title template of the message to log. This property can be used from logging frameworks that supports structured logging like: "{user} says {quote}". In the example, titleTemplate will be this string and title will be "Gilfoyle says It's not magic. It's talent and sweat".

options.name: --source
options.description: The source of the code logging the message. This could be the assembly name.

options.name: --statusCode
options.description: If the message logged relates to a HTTP status code, you can put the code in this property. This would probably only be relevant for errors, but could be used for logging successful status codes as well.

options.name: --dateTime
options.description: The date and time in UTC of the message. If you don't provide us with a value in dateTime, we will set the current date and time in UTC.

options.name: --type
options.description: The type of message. If logging an error, the type of the exception would go into type but you can put anything in there, that makes sense for your domain.

options.name: --user
options.description: An identification of the user triggering this message. You can put the users email address or your user key into this property.

options.name: --severity
options.description: An enum value representing the severity of this message. The following values are allowed: Verbose, Debug, Information, Warning, Error, Fatal.

options.name: --url
options.description: If message relates to a HTTP request, you may send the URL of that request. If you don't provide us with an URL, we will try to find a key named URL in serverVariables.

options.name: --method
options.description: If message relates to a HTTP request, you may send the HTTP method of that request. If you don't provide us with a method, we will try to find a key named REQUEST_METHOD in serverVariables.

options.name: --version
options.description: Versions can be used to distinguish messages from different versions of your software. The value of version can be a SemVer compliant string or any other syntax that you are using as your version numbering scheme.

options.name: --correlationId
options.description: CorrelationId can be used to group similar log messages together into a single discoverable batch. A correlation ID could be a session ID from ASP.NET Core, a unique string spanning multiple microsservices handling the same request, or similar.

options.name: --category
options.description: The category to set on the message. Category can be used to emulate a logger name when created from a logging framework.

options.name: --proxyHost
options.description: A hostname or IP for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

options.name: --proxyPort
options.description: A port number for a proxy to use to call elmah.io

options.name: --to

options.name: --filename

options.name: --query

options.name: --includeHeaders

options.name: --format
options.description: The format to export [default: Json]

options.name: --proxyHost
